description_long: This traffic barricade is a portable, A-frame style barrier commonly used for roadwork, construction, or event crowd control. It features a horizontal rectangular plank at the top, painted white with alternating diagonal orange reflective stripes for high visibility. The plank is supported by two pairs of angled legs, forming a sturdy frame that keeps the barricade upright. The legs are reinforced with horizontal crossbars for added stability. The structure appears to be made primarily of painted wood or composite material, with visible wear and weathering. There are no visible lights, signage, or additional fixtures attached to the barricade.
description: A portable A-frame traffic barricade with a white plank featuring diagonal orange stripes, supported by angled legs and crossbars.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the barricade, with the orange and white striped plank clearly visible and both sets of angled support legs.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the barricade, highlighting the A-frame structure and the depth of the support legs.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the striped plank and the back sides of the support legs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the A-frame profile and support structure visible.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal orange and white stripes, evenly spaced, running across the top plank.
